comment.body:
it doesn't matter if this plan is good 30% of times or 70% of times.
the thing that matters if you cut your losses early and if you are patient with your gains (not trying to hit the jackpot right and there)

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
risk management (precalculating your daily amount of money you will trade with, putting the levels on the charts, making notes on your own by your understanding, set sell orders, and being stick to the plan) is important bcs - as my menthor said - you will have more bad days than good days.
this is a statistical fact.
as said, odds aren't in your favor.

traders spend most of their time in a drawdown.

you make money in the minority of times.
the rest cancle each other out
